articles.title: The Future of Artificial Intelligence: How Machines Are Learning to Think
articles.author: Putera Gani
articles.excerpt: From deep learning breakthroughs to the ethical questions keeping researchers up at night — a deep dive into the technology reshaping civilization as we know it.
articles.content: Artificial intelligence is no longer science fiction. It is reshaping industries, economies, and the very nature of what it means to create.

articles.title: The Principles of Timeless Design That Never Go Out of Style
articles.author: Putera Gani
articles.excerpt: Why Dieter Rams' ten commandments of good design remain as relevant today as they were in 1978.
articles.content: Good design is honest. It does not make a product appear more innovative, powerful or valuable than it really is.

articles.title: Quantum Computing Explained: The Race to Rewrite the Rules of Physics
articles.author: Putera Gani
articles.excerpt: Inside the labs where scientists are harnessing quantum mechanics to build computers of the future.
articles.content: Inside the most advanced physics labs on earth, a new type of computer is being born — one that operates on the strange rules of quantum mechanics.

articles.title: How Cities Shape the Human Mind and the Way We Think
articles.author: Putera Gani
articles.excerpt: The surprising neuroscience behind urban environments and why dense cities make us smarter and more creative.
articles.content: Something happens to the human mind in a city. The density of people, ideas, and accidents creates a cognitive environment unlike anything our ancestors knew.

articles.title: The Web3 Illusion: Separating Hype from Genuine Innovation
articles.author: Putera Gani
articles.excerpt: A critical examination of blockchain promises versus reality, and what genuinely new possibilities it unlocks.
articles.content: The promise of Web3 was revolutionary: a decentralized internet owned by its users, free from corporate gatekeepers.

articles.title: Typography in the Digital Age: Why Letters Still Matter More Than Ever
articles.author: Putera Gani
articles.excerpt: How the craft of type-setting has evolved from Gutenberg's press to responsive variable fonts on modern screens.
articles.content: Before images, before color, before motion — there was text. And the way that text looks shapes how we feel about every word we read.

articles.title: The Climate Crisis and the Innovators Racing to Fix It
articles.author: Putera Gani
articles.excerpt: From direct air capture to fusion reactors — the technologies that could redefine humanity's relationship with the planet.
articles.content: The climate emergency is no longer a future problem. It is today's emergency, demanding today's solutions.

articles.title: The Quiet Revolution in Mental Health Technology
articles.author: Putera Gani
articles.excerpt: Smartphone apps and AI therapists are changing access to mental healthcare — but at what cost to human connection?
articles.content: One in four people will experience a mental health condition in their lifetime. For most of history, the path to treatment was narrow and expensive.

articles.title: Why the Creator Economy Is Rewriting the Rules of Work
articles.author: Putera Gani
articles.excerpt: More than 50 million people now call themselves creators. What happens when passion becomes profession at scale?
articles.content: A generation ago, you needed a studio, a label, a publisher, or a network to reach an audience. Today, you need a phone and an idea.

articles.title: How Personal Branding Is Becoming the New Resume
articles.author: Putera Gani
articles.excerpt: Why reputation and online identity are replacing traditional resumes.
articles.content: A short summary of the article...
